senses.definition: to create a sequence of dance steps, often set to music, for a performance or production

examples.x: She choreographed a beautiful ballet performance for the dance recital.
He choreographed a lively dance routine for the school talent show.
The professional choreographed the intricate dance sequence for the music video.
The ballet company is choreographing a classical piece for their repertoire.
She is choreographing a new dance routine for the upcoming performance.

senses.definition: to dance energetically, especially to rhythm and blues or rock and roll music

examples.x: She loves to boogie on the dance floor whenever her favorite song comes on.
Last night, we boogied until the early hours of the morning.
They are boogieing to the music at the party.
He has boogied at many dance competitions in his career.
Tomorrow, we will boogie at the concert like there's no tomorrow!

senses.definition: to move the body to music in a special way

examples.x: During the carnival, everyone were dancing in the streets.
She loves to dance to her favorite songs.

senses.definition: to dance energetically to disco music characterized by a strong beat and synthesized sound

examples.x: She discoes at the club every Friday night.
Last weekend, they discoed until the early hours of the morning.
They are discoing to their favorite tunes right now.
He has discoed at many parties throughout his life.
He often discoes with his friends when they play disco music.

senses.definition: to dance, move, or skip with quick, lively steps

examples.x: The group of friends decided to jig around the bonfire, adding a festive atmosphere to the celebration.
During the carnival parade, participants dressed in colorful costumes jigged along the route, entertaining spectators.
The children are jigging to the catchy tune playing on the radio.

senses.definition: to dance, specifically in the style of jive dance, characterized by lively and energetic movements

examples.x: They jive together every Saturday night at the local dance club.
Last weekend, they jived to their favorite songs at the party.
They are currently jiving to the upbeat music playing in the background.
They have jived at many dance competitions throughout the years.
Tomorrow, they will be jiving to the latest hits at the concert.

senses.definition: to dance vigorously in a highly energetic and often chaotic manner

examples.x: The crowd moshed wildly to the heavy metal music, creating a pulsating energy.
People were moshing and slamming into each other, caught up in the intensity of the punk rock show.
Despite the heat, the fans moshed enthusiastically, expressing their passion for the band's music.
Even though it was a small venue, the fans still managed to mosh with incredible energy and enthusiasm.
The fans mosh enthusiastically, fully immersed in the energy of the concert.

senses.definition: to dance with lively and vibrant movements, often involving swaying or shaking of the hips or shoulders in a playful or flirtatious manner

examples.x: The dancers shimmied in unison, creating a mesmerizing display of rhythm and motion.
They shimmy playfully, enjoying the carefree atmosphere of the party.
The performers shimmied with infectious energy, drawing cheers from the audience.
He shimmies to the music, adding his own unique flair to the dance routine.

senses.definition: to perform the tango dance, known for its passionate and dramatic movements

examples.x: They tangoed across the dance floor with elegance and precision.
The couple tangoes with intensity, expressing their emotions through every step.
We tangoed to the sultry music, lost in the rhythm of the dance.
He tangoes with confidence, leading his partner with grace and poise.
They have tangoed together for years, perfecting their dance with each performance.

senses.definition: to dance in a sexually provocative manner by rapidly moving and thrusting one's hips and buttocks back and forth
to rapidly and intensely move back and forth, causing vibrations or tremors

examples.x: As the thunder roared, the windows began to shake with the intensity of the storm.

senses.definition: to dance the waltz, a graceful ballroom dance characterized by smooth, gliding movements

examples.x: The couple waltzed across the dance floor with effortless grace.
They waltz together every anniversary, reliving the magic of their wedding day.
She learned to waltz at a young age and has loved the dance ever since.
They waltzed under the stars, lost in the enchantment of the moment.
As the music began, they took each other's hands and waltzed into the night.

senses.definition: to make one's time enjoyable by doing something that is interesting and does not make one bored

examples.x: She amused herself by reading a funny book on her commute.
Playing board games with friends always amuses him on weekends.
The playful antics of the kittens amused the children for hours.
The animated cartoon series amused kids and adults alike.

senses.definition: to introduce something or someone to the public for the first time

examples.x: The singer debuts her new song at the music festival.
The author debuts her novel at the book launch event.
The artist debuted her new collection at the gallery opening.
The fashion designer debuted her spring collection during Fashion Week.
The band debuted their new album on social media last night.

senses.definition: to amuse someone so that they have an enjoyable time

examples.x: The clown entertained the children at the birthday party with magic tricks and balloon animals.
The comedian entertained the audience with jokes and witty anecdotes.
They entertained guests at the dinner party with live music and dancing.
The band will entertain the crowd with their upbeat music.
The magician is entertaining the children with his magic tricks.

senses.definition: to improvise or perform spontaneously without prior preparation or rehearsal

examples.x: The musician extemporized a beautiful melody on the piano, captivating the audience with her creativity.
Without a script, the actor had to extemporize his lines during the improvised scene, relying on his quick thinking and wit.
She extemporizes a new poem every morning, drawing inspiration from her surroundings.
She has extemporized speeches at numerous conferences throughout her career.
They are currently extemporizing ideas for the upcoming debate competition.

senses.definition: to be the star performer in a concert or performance

examples.x: She will headline the music festival this summer, drawing a huge crowd.
The famous band is set to headline the concert at the stadium next weekend.
He headlined the comedy show, delivering a memorable and hilarious performance.
The new artist will headline the tour, marking a significant milestone in their career.
The popular DJ headlined the nightclub event, making it an unforgettable night.

senses.definition: to give a performance of something such as a play or a piece of music for entertainment

examples.x: The band will perform at the city park next week.
The comedian performed in front of a large audience.
The magician performed an incredible trick.
The orchestra will perform Beethoven's Fifth Symphony tonight.
They perform a traditional dance at the festival every year.

senses.definition: to do exercises or activities to get one's body, voice, or mind ready for something, like a workout or a performance
to create and perform words of a play, music, etc. on impulse and without preparation, particularly because one is forced to do so

examples.x: When the actor forgot his lines, he had to improvise to keep the scene going.
Musicians often improvise solos during live performances for added spontaneity.
During the comedy show, the comedian would often improvise jokes based on audience reactions.
The jazz band showcased their ability to improvise by creating unique melodies on the spot.
Unable to find his notes, the speaker improvised a captivating speech on the spot.

senses.definition: to attend or participate in a rave, a large, lively party or event characterized by electronic music, dancing, and a vibrant atmosphere

examples.x: Last weekend, they raved at the music festival, dancing until the early hours of the morning.
They are currently raving at the club, immersed in the pulsating beats and colorful lights.
Having raved at many parties before, he considers himself quite the connoisseur of electronic music.
Tomorrow night, they will rave at the beach party, looking forward to dancing under the stars.
